title: Temporal versus target leakage
description: How temporal leakage (future data in a row) and target leakage (label-derived features) differ and how each is detected.
body: Use when distinguishing the two main leakage classes during a review. Temporal leakage uses feature values recorded after the label time, fixed by point-in-time joins; target leakage uses features computed from or after the outcome, fixed by dropping those fields. Do not conflate them — the remedies differ. Actions: for temporal leakage audit join timing; for target leakage trace feature provenance to the outcome. Validation signals include a join-timing audit and a feature-provenance trace with each fix recorded. Risk boundary: either leakage class ships a model that fails on real data; if a feature's relationship to the label is ambiguous, escalate and exclude it pending review.
